risk_title: Political and Governance Instability Affecting Project Implementation
risk_summary: Political and governance risks remain at high level, directly impacting the project's ability to implement reforms and maintain institutional capacity for the state-owned banks modernization agenda.
evidence_quote: Political and Governance High High High

risk_title: COVID-19 Driven Fund Cancellation and Project Scope Reduction
risk_summary: Substantial cancellation of 70% of project funds ($28 million) in response to government's COVID-19 emergency response has forced significant project restructuring, reducing scope to focus only on bank merger while dropping IT investments and other activities.
evidence_quote: The project is due to substantial restructuring a result of the cancellation of US$28 million (70 percent of project funds) in support of government's COVID-19 emergency response. As a result, the project's scope will need to be significantly reduced focusing on the support with the merger of New Kabul Bank (NKB) and Bank-e-Millie Afghan (BMA) while dropping the bulk of other activities such as previously planned IT investments.

risk_title: Deteriorating Service Delivery and Client Outreach Due to COVID-19
risk_summary: COVID-19 impact on service delivery has caused continued decline in state bank client numbers, with outreach falling from baseline of 2,206 thousand clients to 1,766 thousand, moving away from the target of 3,200 thousand clients.
evidence_quote: This trend has continued over the reporting period due to the impact of Covid-19 on service deliver.

risk_title: Institutional Capacity Constraints for Implementation and Sustainability
risk_summary: Institutional capacity for implementation and sustainability has been downgraded from Substantial to High risk, indicating weakening ability of implementing agencies to execute project activities effectively.
evidence_quote: Institutional Capacity for Implementation and Sustainability Substantial Substantial High

risk_summary: Fiduciary risk rating has increased from Substantial to High, indicating heightened concerns about financial management and accountability in project implementation.

risk_title: Governance Compliance Delays in Board Appointments
risk_summary: Board member appointments remain incomplete with 2 members still pending final approval from the central bank (DAB), delaying full compliance with OECD governance guidelines and affecting institutional stability.
evidence_quote: Board composition: Five member board in place in two banks, one bank only has three members and two more are recruited, but they are still pending final approval from DAB.
